module-4-customer-cleanup-project.label: Module 4 Customer Cleanup Project
module-4-customer-cleanup-project.summary: Build a customer cleanup worksheet with text, list, and NULL checks.

mp-1-find-missing-emails.title: Find the missing email records
mp-1-find-missing-emails.prompt: Begin the Module 4 Customer Cleanup Project. Return `id, full_name, email` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `email IS NULL`.
mp-1-find-missing-emails.hint: Switch the NULL check so it keeps the missing values.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, full_name, email` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `email IS NULL`.

mp-1-find-missing-emails.starterCode:
-- Returning id, full_name, and email from customers where email is NULL.
SELECT
  id,
  full_name,
  email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NOT NULL;

mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.title: Keep the Inactive Follow-Ups
mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.prompt: Extend the Module 4 Customer Cleanup Project with the next approved requirement. Return `id, full_name, email, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `email IS NULL AND status = 'inactive'`.
mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.hint: Add an exact status condition for `inactive`.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, full_name, email, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `email IS NULL AND status = 'inactive'`.

mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.starterCode:
-- Keep the missing-email rule above.
-- Add status and keep inactive customers.
SELECT
  id,
  full_name,
  email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NULL;

mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.title: Finish the cleanup report
mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.prompt: Complete the Module 4 Customer Cleanup Project. Return `id, full_name, email, city, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `email IS NULL AND status = 'inactive' AND city LIKE 'H%' AND is_test = 0`.
mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.hint: Add `city`, keep cities beginning with `H`, and require `is_test = 0`.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, full_name, email, city, status` from `customers` in that column order. Keep only rows where `email IS NULL AND status = 'inactive' AND city LIKE 'H%' AND is_test = 0`.

mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.starterCode:
-- Keep the filters above.
-- Add city, exclude test rows, and keep only cities that start with H.
SELECT
  id,
  full_name,
  email,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NULL
  AND status = 'inactive';

try_module_4_customer_cleanup_project_sk.title: Preview Missing-Email Records
try_module_4_customer_cleanup_project_sk.prompt: Before adding the remaining cleanup rules, find the records with no stored email. Return `id`, `full_name`, and `email` from `customers` where `email` is NULL.

module-4-customer-cleanup-project.label: Module 4 Customer Cleanup Project
module-4-customer-cleanup-project.summary: Build a customer cleanup worksheet with text, list, and NULL checks.

mp-1-find-missing-emails.title: Find the missing email records
mp-1-find-missing-emails.prompt: Start the cleanup report by returning `id`, `full_name`, and `email` from `customers` where `email` is NULL.
mp-1-find-missing-emails.hint: Switch the NULL check so it keeps the missing values.

help.concept: Cleanup queries often start by isolating the rows that are missing required information.
help.hint_2: Run the query and confirm the results table shows only customers without email values.

mp-1-find-missing-emails.starterCode:
-- Returning id, full_name, and email from customers where email is NULL.
SELECT
  id,
  full_name,
  email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NOT NULL;

mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.title: Keep the Inactive Follow-Ups
mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.prompt: Add `status` to the result, and keep only customers whose status is `inactive`.
mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.hint: Add an exact status condition for `inactive`.

help.concept: The status rule should preserve the missing-email records that still require cleanup.
help.hint_2: Add `status` to the SELECT list and `status IN ('active', 'draft')` to the WHERE clause.

mp-2-keep-real-follow-ups.starterCode:
-- Keep the missing-email rule above.
-- Add status and keep inactive customers.
SELECT
  id,
  full_name,
  email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NULL;

mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.title: Finish the cleanup report
mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.prompt: Add `city` to the result, exclude test rows, and keep only customers whose city starts with `H`.
mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.hint: Add `city`, keep cities beginning with `H`, and require `is_test = 0`.

help.concept: The final step combines missing-data, status, text-pattern, and test-record checks in one query.
help.hint_2: Use `city LIKE 'Chi%'` and `is_test = 0` while keeping the earlier conditions.

mp-3-finish-cleanup-report.starterCode:
-- Keep the filters above.
-- Add city, exclude test rows, and keep only cities that start with H.
SELECT
  id,
  full_name,
  email,
  status
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NULL
  AND status = 'inactive';

try-module-4-customer-cleanup-project-sk.title: Preview Missing-Email Records
try-module-4-customer-cleanup-project-sk.prompt: Before adding the remaining cleanup rules, find the records with no stored email. Return `id`, `full_name`, and `email` from `customers` where `email` is NULL.
try-module-4-customer-cleanup-project-sk.hint: Use `IS NULL` so the preview keeps records with a missing email.

help.concept: This warm-up isolates the missing-email records that the cleanup project will refine.
help.hint_1: Check the selected columns and table name in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the result table with the expected result.

try-module-4-customer-cleanup-project-sk.starterCode:
-- Return id, full_name, email from the customers table.
SELECT
  id,
  full_name,
  email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NOT NULL;

try_module_4_customer_cleanup_project_sk.title: Preview Missing-Email Records
try_module_4_customer_cleanup_project_sk.prompt: Before adding the remaining cleanup rules, find the records with no stored email. Return `id`, `full_name`, and `email` from `customers` where `email` is NULL.

sketch_project_brief.title: Project brief
sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
A customer-data audit needs a short worksheet of records with missing contact information.

Start with missing emails, confirm the unresolved status, then narrow the list by location and test-data rules.

The finished query should return a real cleanup target instead of an empty result.

sketch_project_brief.bodyMarkdown:
